# Claude Code Agent Teams: Orchestrate Multiple Claude Sessions in Parallel

[![Algo Insights](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:32:32/1*kcZSvDRaxmbCAUerJnPTUw.png)](https://medium.com/@algoinsights?source=post_page---byline--98eb6d14513e---------------------------------------)

[Algo Insights](https://medium.com/@algoinsights?source=post_page---byline--98eb6d14513e---------------------------------------)

Follow

15 min read

·

Feb 8, 2026

71

[Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3D98eb6d14513e&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fcoding-nexus%2Fclaude-code-agent-teams-orchestrate-multiple-claude-sessions-in-parallel-98eb6d14513e&source=---header_actions--98eb6d14513e---------------------post_audio_button------------------)

Share

You’re refactoring a complex codebase. The changes span the API layer, database migrations, test coverage, and documentation. A single Claude Code session handles one piece at a time, progressing through the work sequentially.

You try subagents. They parallelize execution, but each works in isolation. They report results back to you, yet they can’t share findings, challenge assumptions, or coordinate directly. When you need real collaboration among AI workers — not just parallel execution — subagents hit a wall.

Agent Teams solves this problem.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/1*7YOmTnlEuWjj_FpU0VehTw.png)

Enable Claude Code Agent Teams and orchestrate a collaborative team with one prompt:

```
# Add to your environment or settings.json
export CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1
```

Then tell Claude:

```
Create an agent team to refactor the payment module. Spawn three teammates:
one for the API layer, one for the database migrations, one for test coverage.
Have them coordinate through the shared task list.
```

Claude creates a team lead, spawns three independent teammates, and coordinates their work via a shared task list and direct messaging. Each teammate owns their scope. No conflicts, isolation, or bottlenecks.

## What Are Claude Code Agent Teams?

Agent Teams is an experimental feature that lets you orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions collaborating on a shared project. One session serves as the team lead, coordinating work, assigning tasks, and synthesising results. Teammates work independently, each in its own context window, and communicate directly with one another.

## Why Agent Teams Matter Now

Anthropic shipped Agent Teams as an experimental feature with the Opus 4.6 release. The community had been independently building similar patterns for months, using tools such as OpenClaw and custom orchestration scripts. Anthropic recognized the demand and built it into Claude Code natively.

This is significant for three reasons:

**Native integration beats bolted-on solutions.** The shared task list, mailbox system, and teammate lifecycle…
